name: ruff
description: Ruff: An extremely fast Python linter

name: -v
--verbose
options.description: Enable verbose logging

name: -q
--quiet
options.description: Print diagnostics, but nothing else

name: -s
--silent
options.description: Disable all logging (but still exit with status code "1" upon detecting diagnostics)

options.name: --config
options.description: Path to the `pyproject.toml` or `ruff.toml` file to use for configuration

options.name: --isolated
options.description: Ignore all configuration files

options.name: --help
options.description: Print help

subcommands.name: check
subcommands.description: Run Ruff on the given files or directories

args.name: Path
args.description: The path to use for the project/script

options.name: --fix
options.description: Apply fixes to resolve lint violations

options.name: --unsafe-fixes
options.description: Include fixes that may not retain the original intent of the code

options.name: --show-fixes
options.description: Show an enumeration of all fixed lint violations

options.name: --diff
options.description: Avoid writing any fixed files back; instead, output a diff for each changed file to stdout, and exit 0 if there are no diffs. Implies `--fix-only`

name: -w
--watch
options.description: Run in watch mode by re-running whenever files change

options.name: --fix-only
options.description: Apply fixes to resolve lint violations, but don't report on, or exit non-zero for, leftover violations. Implies `--fix`

options.name: --ignore-noqa
options.description: Ignore any `# noqa` comments

options.name: --output-format
options.description: Output serialization format for violations. The default serialization format is 'full'

name: -o
--output-file
options.description: Specify file to write the linter output to (default: stdout)

options.name: --target-version
options.description: The minimum Python version that should be supported

options.name: --preview
options.description: Enable preview mode; checks will include unstable rules and fixes

options.name: --extension
options.description: List of mappings from file extension to language (one of `python`, `ipynb`, `pyi`). For example, to treat `.ipy` files as IPython notebooks, use `--extension ipy:ipynb`

options.name: --statistics
options.description: Show counts for every rule with at least one violation

options.name: --add-noqa
options.description: Enable automatic additions of `noqa` directives to failing lines

options.name: --show-files
options.description: See the files Ruff will be run against with the current settings

options.name: --show-settings
options.description: See the settings Ruff will use to lint a given Python file

name: -h
--help
options.description: Print help

options.name: --select
options.description: Comma-separated list of rule codes to enable (or ALL, to enable all rules)

options.name: --ignore
options.description: Comma-separated list of rule codes to disable

options.name: --extend-select
options.description: Like --select, but adds additional rule codes on top of the selected ones

options.name: --per-file-ignores
options.description: List of mappings from file pattern to code to exclude

options.name: --extend-per-file-ignores
options.description: Like `--per-file-ignores`, but adds additional ignores on top of those already specified

options.name: --fixable
options.description: List of rule codes to treat as eligible for fix. Only applicable when fix itself is enabled (e.g., via `--fix`)

options.name: --unfixable
options.description: List of rule codes to treat as ineligible for fix. Only applicable when fix itself is enabled (e.g., via `--fix`)

options.name: --extend-fixable
options.description: Like --fixable, but adds additional rule codes on top of those already specified

options.name: --exclude
options.description: List of paths, used to omit files and/or directories from analysis

options.name: --extend-exclude
options.description: Like --exclude, but adds additional files and directories on top of those already excluded

options.name: --respect-gitignore
options.description: Respect file exclusions via `.gitignore` and other standard ignore files

options.name: --force-exclude
options.description: Enforce exclusions, even for paths passed to Ruff directly on the command-line

name: -n
--no-cache
options.description: Disable cache reads

options.name: --cache-dir
options.description: Path to the cache directory

options.name: --stdin-filename
options.description: The name of the file when passing it through stdin

name: -e
--exit-zero
options.description: Exit with status code "0", even upon detecting lint violations

options.name: --exit-non-zero-on-fix
options.description: Exit with a non-zero status code if any files were modified via fix, even if no lint violations remain

options.name: --all
options.description: Explain all rules

options.name: --output-format
options.description: Output format [default: text]

subcommands.name: config
subcommands.description: List or describe the available configuration options

options.name: --output-format
options.description: Output format [default: text]

options.name: --output-format
options.description: Output format

subcommands.name: clean
subcommands.description: Clear any caches in the current directory and any subdirectories

subcommands.name: format
subcommands.description: Run the Ruff formatter on the given files or directories

args.name: Path
args.description: List of files or directories to format [default: .]

options.name: --check
options.description: Avoid writing any formatted files back; instead, exit with a non-zero status code if any files would have been modified, and zero otherwise

options.name: --diff
options.description: Avoid writing any formatted files back; instead, exit with a non-zero status code and the difference between the current file and how the formatted file would look like

options.name: --extension
options.description: List of mappings from file extension to language (one of `python`, `ipynb`, `pyi`). For example, to treat `.ipy` files as IPython notebooks, use `--extension ipy:ipynb`

options.name: --target-version
options.description: The minimum Python version that should be supported

options.name: --preview
options.description: Enable preview mode; enables unstable formatting. Use `--no-preview` to disable

name: -n
--no-cache
options.description: Disable cache reads (env: RUFF_NO_CACHE=)

options.name: --cache-dir
options.description: Path to the cache directory (env: RUFF_CACHE_DIR=)

options.name: --stdin-filename
options.description: The name of the file when passing it through stdin

options.name: --respect-gitignore
options.description: Respect file exclusions via `.gitignore` and other standard ignore files

options.name: --exclude
options.description: List of paths, used to omit files and/or directories from analysis

options.name: --force-exclude
options.description: Enforce exclusions, even for paths passed to Ruff directly on the command-line

options.name: --line-length
options.description: Set the line-length

options.name: --range
options.description: When specified, Ruff will try to only format the code in the given range

options.name: --preview
options.description: Enable preview mode. Use `--no-preview` to disable

options.name: --no-preview
options.description: Disable preview mode

subcommands.name: analyze
subcommands.description: Run analysis over Python source code

subcommands.name: graph
subcommands.description: Generate a map of Python file dependencies or dependents

args.name: Path
args.description: The path to use for the project/script

options.name: --direction
options.description: The direction of the import map. By default, generates a dependency map, i.e., a map from file to files that it depends on. Use `--direction dependents` to generate a map from file to files that depend on it

options.name: --detect-string-imports
options.description: Attempt to detect imports from string literals

options.name: --preview
options.description: Enable preview mode. Use `--no-preview` to disable

options.name: --no-preview
options.description: Disable preview mode

options.name: --target-version
options.description: The minimum Python version that should be supported

options.name: --output-format
options.description: Output format

subcommands.name: help
subcommands.description: Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)
